title: Case Official – Credit Collections & Support Team – 6 Month Fixed Term Contract
description: Case Official \- Credit Collections \& Support Team \- 6 Month Fixed Term Contract At IVC Evidensia, we are a leading veterinary services provider in Europe and North America with more than 2,500 clinics and hospitals across 19 countries and around 42,000 employees. We aim to have an extraordinary impact on animal lives, supporting in the region of 25,000 animals every single day across our international network. IVC Evidensia exists to champion the long\-term health and welfare of animals and deliver our purpose of Healthy Animals, Happy Owners. At IVC Evidensia, our Central Collections team plays a vital role in supporting our veterinary practices and clients by managing outstanding balances in a fair, professional, and customer\-focused way. As a Case Official, you will be responsible for managing client accounts, engaging with clients through inbound and outbound contact, agreeing appropriate repayment solutions, and resolving cases efficiently while maintaining high standards of service, compliance, and empathy. This is an entry\-level role and a great opportunity for someone looking to build experience in a structured, supportive collections or customer contact environment, with clear development pathways into senior and leadership roles. **This role is being offered on a 6\-month fixed term contract basis.** Key Accountabilities/Responsibilities: **Client \& Case Management** * Manage client accounts through inbound and outbound communication channels (phone, email, letters). * Engage with clients to understand their circumstances and agree appropriate repayment solutions. * Resolve assigned cases through standard processes, ensuring timely and accurate outcomes. * Maintain a professional, empathetic, and customer\-focused approach in all interactions. **Operational Delivery** * Work to agreed treatment strategies, policies, and processes to deliver consistent outcomes. * Meet individual productivity, quality, and compliance targets. * Maintain accurate and complete case notes, records, and documentation. * Escalate complex, sensitive, or vulnerable cases appropriately to Senior Case Officials or Team Leaders. **Compliance, Risk \& Quality** * Adhere to Debt Management Policy, Client Vulnerability Policy, Data\-Protection Policy and standards, and complaints procedures as well as our IVC Code of Responsibility. * Identify and flag potential vulnerability indicators and follow agreed support processes. * Participate in quality assurance activities and respond positively to feedback and coaching. * Maintain auditable records and follow required controls and procedures. **Team \& Continuous Improvement** * Participate in training, coaching, and team development activities. * Contribute to a positive team culture focused on accountability, learning, and continuous improvement. * Share feedback and ideas to improve client experience and operational effectiveness. Experience/Qualifications: **Essential:** * Experience in a customer service, contact\-centre, collections, or client\-facing role. * Confident communicator with the ability to handle sensitive or challenging conversations calmly and professionally. * Strong attention to detail with the ability to follow processes and maintain accurate records. * Basic numeracy and literacy skills, with confidence using systems and technology. * Willingness to learn, develop, and take on feedback. **Desirable:** * Previous experience in collections * Experience working in a regulated or client\-sensitive environment (e.g. healthcare, financial services, utilities). * Awareness of vulnerability or affordability considerations (training will be provided). Following receipt of your application, you will be contacted by one of our experienced hire recruitment team. **As a BAME and LGBTQ\+ inclusive employer, we are keen to hear from candidates from all minority and diverse groups. As a Disability Confident Employer, we are keen to hear from candidates with disabilities and long\-term health conditions and would be happy to discuss any reasonable adjustments needed during the recruitment process.** **What We Offer:** At IVC Evidensia we recognise that our people are fundamental to the success of our business. Investing in our people, premises and processes is at the heart of what we do. In addition to a competitive base salary, you will benefit from * Work\-life balance * 26 days annual leave * Cycle to Work scheme * Initiatives focused on employee wellbeing * Pension * Discretionary Bonus * Discounted staff pet care * Free Parking available at Head Office **At IVC Evidensia we are a committed to Diversity, Equality, Inclusion and Belonging, we are keen to hear from candidates from all minority and diverse groups. As a Disability Confident Employer, we are keen to hear from candidates with disabilities and long\-term health conditions and would be happy to discuss any reasonable adjustments needed during the recruitment process.** We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Chef de Partie
description: **Job Title: Chef de Partie** **Your responsibilities** As a Chef de Partie, your responsibilities will include: * Overseeing the preparation and presentation of food in your designated section. * Ensuring consistency and quality of dishes, adhering to recipes and restaurant standards. * Managing the daily operations of your section, including stock rotation and mise en place. * Collaborating with the kitchen team to ensure efficient service during busy periods. * Maintaining cleanliness and hygiene standards in your area, including food safety regulations. * Training and supervising junior kitchen staff, providing guidance and feedback. * Assisting the Head Chef with menu planning and daily specials. * Contributing to the development of new recipes and seasonal menu updates. * Monitoring and managing kitchen inventory, ensuring proper stock levels. * Handling any issues in the kitchen promptly, ensuring smooth service and guest satisfaction. **Requirements** * **Proven experience** as a Chef de Partie or Demi Chef de Partie in a similar role in a high\-volume kitchen. * **Strong culinary skills** with an in\-depth knowledge of food preparation, cooking techniques, and presentation. * **Ability to work under pressure** in a fast\-paced environment while maintaining high standards. * **Excellent organizational skills** to manage time efficiently and handle multiple tasks simultaneously. * **Good knowledge of food safety** and hygiene practices, with a commitment to maintaining a clean and safe kitchen environment. * **Team player** with the ability to collaborate effectively with other kitchen staff. * **Creative mindset** with a passion for food and innovation in menu development. * **Excellent communication skills** to ensure smooth coordination between kitchen and front\-of\-house teams. * **Flexibility** to work various shifts, including nights, weekends, and holidays. Pay: €28,169\.76\-€38,062\.29 per year Benefits: * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Client Associate - Spark Foundry
description: **The Role** **Overview** Media Teams are our client facing teams in Core responsible for excellence in client service, leading strategy development and managing the media plan. The purpose of this role is to support the Client Managers and Senior Client Managers in the day to day running of client accounts; and handle the administration of excellent media delivery across many clients. It requires the use of media planning tools, the effective management of the media planning processes, excellent attention to detail for accurate reporting and time management to ensure best in class delivery on behalf of the media team for clients. It also requires strong collaboration and communication with the wider organisation across the Central Team to ensure that delivery is to the highest standards. A strong working relationship with colleagues, particularly the Channel Planning and Investment teams, is a crucial element of this role. Media Team Client Associates have a very structured learning programme in place, delivered by the best trainers in the industry. The purpose of this is to ensure a consistent level of knowledge covering everything from the basics to advanced areas of media planning. Quality control of output will be critical to this role. **Responsibilities** Central to this role is a clear understanding of media planning principles and an ability to interpret plans at a high level. This role is central to the efficient and effective running of the day\-to\-day client service team, including working with the Client Manager to help with the actualisation of media plans, monitoring of POs, timely and accurate billing, using agency tools to develop audience and competitive reporting, creating presentations of competitor insights, ideation and working with the team to deliver innovation. As part of this, our Client Associates will have a specific role in helping to execute each team’s Client Satisfaction Plans, which would be defined depending on the client team. The Client Associate will work closely with the Channel Planning and Investment teams, will manage the delivery of the media plan, and will ensure that it is aligned with the overall media rationale. This will all be delivered accurately and by adhering to processes as outlined in our ways of working. KPIs will include unbilled %, efficient resolution of media queries, accuracy of reporting and “Delivery” client service scores in the annual RADAR service review. **AGENCY PERFORMANCE** * Generating reports that deliver actionable insight for clients e.g. using tools such as Adintel, TGI, Techedge etc. * Listens actively to understand client needs * Assisting in the creation and development world class media strategies that can be turned into media plans that are based on best practice planning as taught through Core Learning * Working with the Client Manager on the management of campaign from start to end, essentially project managing the campaign (dealing with client queries, coordinating reports, monitoring, ensuring plans are audit ready, liaising with the digital specialists to monitor campaign delivery, etc) * Learning about all media channels and the rate at which they contribute to the overall plan Internal coordination with Core Practices and Central Teams on live campaign queries including delivery updates, trans times and pacing reports * Update clients on key peak spots in launch week of activity * Develop reports on both routine and ad hoc basis, as well as implementing recommendations from Client Manager/Senior Client Manager based on the data * Liaising with the Creative Agency and OOH agencies for delivery of creative assets * Develop an understanding of each Practice and Central Team within Core and what it contributes to the overall plan * Validate campaign delivery and performance * Help to project manage post\-campaign reviews for clients, as well as populate information into client\-friendly presentation * Helping prep media presentations * Helping to coordinate media owner briefs and delivery * Working with the Client Manager to ensure all plans are audit ready each month * Adopt and embrace an accuracy culture by having and supporting excellent attention to detail * Where required, screenshots of digital activity, or page positions in press may also need to be monitored and reported on **COMMERCIAL PERFORMANCE** * Helping with the collection and reconciliation of client ensuring effective and efficient month end billing in conjunction with the Media Operations Associate * Helping with reconciliation of all media plans at month end to reflect delivery * Main responsibility for billing at the end of each month (reviewed by team) * Ensure all billing is 100% accurate and on time for clients, and particularly on new out of scope projects, with correct booking category being used to easily and accurately identify progress. * Ensure timesheets are updated weekly and that they accurately reflect the time spent on our clients’ businesses. **TALENT MANAGEMENT** * Commit to the internal Core Learning programme(s) and the defined training track to be completed in your first year; and regularly attend training for your development proactively, outside of formal scheduled training time * Helping to drive and build culture, creating a fun working and learning environment * Collaborate closely with the Client Director to ensure your personal development requirements are met * Ensure a strong comprehension and commitment to your career development plan in line with your 360 appraisal, quarterly catch up’s and goals * Uphold Core’s values in words and actions * Proactively build relationships within the wider company **Your profile** **Experience** * No previous experience of media planning is required but an interest in media planning and buying is essential * Good working knowledge of Microsoft Office suite, particularly Excel and PowerPoint * High level of business acumen **Person Specification** * Be energetic and efficient in approach * Actively display a passion for the industry * Strong attention to detail * Show curiosity in learning new things at a fast pace every day * Excellent interpersonal skills\- ability to build strong relationships with colleagues across Core, clients and media owners * Display the ability to think innovatively * Understands the importance of upskilling * Understands and appreciates the benefit of processes * Be responsive to requests * Know when to escalate and involve other people **Why us?** **What’s in it for you?** * Work your way – enjoy flexible, hybrid working that fits around your life * Never stop growing – 24/7 access to LinkedIn Learning, plus clear career progression and mentoring support * Variety that excites – collaborate with an incredible mix of clients across sport, retail, food \& drink, alcohol, insurance, banking and more * Grow with us – real opportunities to develop and progress within the business * Time to recharge – 21 days annual leave, your birthday off, Christmas closure days, and early finishes on bank holiday Fridays * Balance matters – a culture that genuinely supports work–life balance * People\-first perks – employee referral programme and family\-focused benefits, including paid maternity \& paternity leave, fertility support, pregnancy loss leave, and a maternity phase\-back programme * Your wellbeing, covered – access to mental health and wellbeing programmes, plus paid health insurance for you and your dependents under 18 * Plan for the future – professional financial advice, contributory pension, and life assurance * Be part of something bigger – join our D\&I, CSR, Green, Social or Great Place to Work committees and even earn an extra day’s leave for making an impact * And plenty more along the way… **Salary Range** 30000 \- 31000 DOE **About Us** Core is Ireland's largest marketing communications company with over 400 people working across the key areas of marketing communications \- research, data science, strategy, planning, creative development and implementation. We are now a collective of nine practices, working towards a single purpose \- to expand the possibilities of what brands can achieve. At Core, we believe that change can move us forward and compel us towards new possibilities. Our wonderful people are at the centre of our success. It is essential that we keep building a high\-trust working environment that supports our people and enables them to flourish. There is no magic formula for becoming a great place to work. Instead, it involves working together, through constant change, to match the personal dreams and ambitions of our people with the aspirations of the organisation. Over the years, we have cemented our position as a great place to work, with 2025 being voted 8 Best Large Workplace in Ireland. Additionally, we are recognised as a Best Workplace for Women, and Best Workplace for Health \& Wellbeing. 2025 was our best year yet, and we're excited to have you join us and our continued success.

title: Cloud Infrastructure Support Engineer
description: ### **Description** Arthur Cox LLP is one of Ireland’s leading law firms. We are an “all\-island” firm with offices in Dublin and Belfast. We also have offices in London, New York, and San Francisco. Our practice encompasses all aspects of corporate and business law. With 600 legal staff and a total headcount of nearly 900, we provide a comprehensive service to an international client base ranging from multinational organisations, banks, and financial institutions and established global leaders to government agencies and new players in emerging industry sectors. Our story has been defined by our progressive outlook. For over 100 years, we have led the way in the legal field in Ireland. For our clients, we work to deliver the best service and support. With an independent, entrepreneurial spirit of innovation leading the way. **Department:** IT Team **Location:** Dublin (Hybrid Model, minimum 3 days onsite) **Reports to:** Head of IT Operations **Contract Type:** Permanent / Full\-time **Background:** Arthur Cox is at the forefront of technology adoption that drive value within the firm, its client's business, and the wider legal industry. IT operations play a central role, connecting digital capabilities to the firm’s objectives and creating a solid foundation for continued success. We are now seeking to hire an experienced Cloud Support Engineer to join our growing IT operations team. **About the Role:** The successful candidate will be responsible for the operational stability, automation, and continuous improvement of the organisation’s cloud platforms. This role has a strong emphasis on Infrastructure as Code (IaC), automation‑first operations, and cloud reliability engineering principles. While the primary focus is Azure infrastructure and platform operations, the role also provides supporting expertise across Microsoft 365 services where they intersect with identity, security, automation, and cloud governance. The successful candidate will work collaboratively across multidisciplinary IT teams including cloud, security, service desk, and applications to deliver stable, secure, and scalable infrastructure services. This role also involves direct interaction with managed service providers to support smooth business operations and ongoing digital transformation initiatives.### **Key Responsibilities** **Cloud Operations:*** Provide operational support for cloud infrastructure across production and non‑production environment. * Monitor platform health, availability, and security, proactively identifying and resolving issues. * Act as a second/third\-line escalation point for complex Azure‑related incidents and participate in root‑cause analysis and service improvement activities. * Support hybrid Azure environments, connectivity, and identity integration using Entra ID **Infrastructure as Code (IaC):*** Design, implement, and maintain Azure infrastructure using Infrastructure as Code. * Ensure environments are repeatable, version‑controlled, and consistent across development, test, and production. * Improve and maintain reusable IaC modules, standards, and patterns aligned with security and governance requirements. **Automation \& DevOps:*** Build automation using PowerShell, Azure CLI, and CI/CD pipelines to reduce manual operational effort. * Integrate infrastructure provisioning and configuration into Azure DevOps or GitHub Actions pipelines. * Automate operational tasks such as patching, scaling, backup, and recovery where appropriate. **Microsoft 365 \& Identity Services:*** Provide operational support for Microsoft 365 services as they relate to cloud identity, security, and automation, including: Entra ID (Azure AD), Conditional Access, MFA Identity lifecycle management and access controls* Collaborate with M365 and endpoint teams to support service reliability and secure configuration. * Assist with automation, monitoring, and incident response impacting M365 services (e.g. authentication, access, service health). * Support integration between Azure platform services and Microsoft 365 where required. **Incident, Problem \& Change Management:*** Manage and resolve incidents within defined SLAs, ensuring high\-quality updates and communication. * Follow ITIL\-driven processes for change, problem, and service performance management. * Participate in root\-cause analysis and continuous improvement activities. **Security, Compliance \& Governance:*** Ensure cloud resources comply with security baselines, governance standards, and regulatory requirements. * Implement and operate Azure security controls including RBAC, Azure Policy, Key Vault, and Defender for Cloud in partnership with the wider infrastructure and security operations team. * Remediate vulnerabilities, apply patches, and support Zero Trust principles where applicable. * Support vulnerability remediation and secure configuration baselines in partnership with security operations. * Contribute to backup, disaster recovery, and business continuity planning for Azure workloads. Ensure compliance with organisational, regulatory, and security standards. * **Documentation \& Continuous Improvement:*** Contribute to the ongoing design and evolution of Azure landing zones and platform services. * Implement and maintain Azure\-native services such as Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, Defender for Cloud, Azure Policy, and Azure Backup. * Support cost management initiatives (FinOps), including tagging, budget controls, and optimisation recommendations. * Identify opportunities to improve resilience, security, and operational efficiency. * Develop and maintain technical documentation, runbooks, and operational standards for Azure platforms. * Identify opportunities to simplify, standardise, and modernise cloud operations using automation and IaC. Participate in knowledge sharing and continuous improvement initiatives within the cloud operations team. * **Project Delivery \& Continuous Improvement:*** Support the delivery of infrastructure projects, upgrades, and migrations. * Contribute to infrastructure modernisation initiatives, including cloud adoption/migration. * Identify opportunities for automation, optimisation, and simplification. * Develop and maintain technical documentation, support guidelines, and runbooks. ### **Skills Knowledge and Expertise** * Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology or related field AND * 5 years’ experience in cloud or infrastructure support, with strong hands\-on Azure operations experience. * Proven experience with implementing and operating Infrastructure as Code in production environments using Terraform and/or Azure Bicep. * Certifications (Highly desirable): Azure Administrator Associate Azure Solutions Architect Expert Microsoft 365 Certified: Administrator Expert ITIL Foundation or similar service management awareness. CISSP or similar security certification a plus * Experience with device analytics or monitoring tools **Essential Technical skills:*** Strong hands\-on experience supporting Microsoft Azure in a production environment. * Strong scripting and automation skills (PowerShell, Azure CLI). * Experience with Azure services such as: Compute (VMs, VMSS) Networking (VNETs, NSGs, VPN / ExpressRoute, Azure Firewall) Storage (Blob, File, Backup) Identity (Azure AD, RBAC, Managed Identities) * Solid understanding of Azure identity, networking, security, and monitoring. * Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines (Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions or similar). * Experience with monitoring and logging tools (Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, Application Insights). * Strong experience with Microsoft 365: Teams, OneDrive, Exchange Online, Intune and Entra ID. * Knowledge of ITIL processes (Incident, Problem, Change Management). **Soft skills:*** Demonstrated critical thinking, troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills to complex technical problems. * Excellent interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills in English to both technical and business audiences (individual or group) * Strong customer service mindset. * High\-quality documentation skills and process\-oriented approach. * Ability to complete assignments with minimal direction. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Strong attention to detail and a methodical approach. * **Additional competencies:*** Collaborative mindset and who can work independently or more often as part of a diverse team and group of internal and external stakeholders. * Exceptional aptitude for dealing with ambiguity, unexpected emergencies, frequent interruptions, and competing deadlines (must be able to work overtime and weekends when necessary). * Proactive, flexible and adaptable disposition with a willingness to work hands\-on, demonstrating attention to detail and the ability to take ownership of individual and team’s work. * Innate curiosity and willingness to learn and evolving technology environment on an ongoing basis, develops knowledge base and skill set beyond that called for by immediate tasks and continually push the ‘knowledge envelope’. * A continuous improvement mindset, continually critically evaluating results to drive improvements of processes and / or methods and/or underlying technology direction. ### **Benefits** We reward, encourage and support our talented people to reach their full potential offering excellent progression opportunities along with a market leading remuneration package and benefits including:* Competitive salary * 25 days annual leave * Hybrid working policy * Immediate pension scheme * Comprehensive health and wellness benefits including full gym and wellness programme * TaxSaver and Bike to Work scheme * Wide\-ranging opportunities for involvement in internal committees ### **About Arthur Cox** Arthur Cox is one of Ireland’s leading law firms and provides a comprehensive service to an international client base ranging from multinational organisations, banks and financial institutions, established global leaders to government agencies and new players in emerging industry sectors. Our story has been defined by our progressive outlook. For over 100 years, the firm has led the way in the legal field in Ireland, delivering the best service and support for our clients. With an independent, entrepreneurial spirit of innovation leading the way. *Arthur Cox LLP is an equal opportunities employer. We value diversity and encourage applications from all sections of society. Please advise if you have any additional requirements, we will happily assist. Any information disclosed by you in this regard will be kept strictly confidential and shared within the firm only as necessary and with your consent.* *Reasonable accommodations provided for the purposes of your interview or which you may require during your employment will not be taken into account in hiring decisions.*

title: Coca-Cola Ignite Intern - PACS
description: **Job Description** **Function Overview at Coca\-Cola** Public Affairs, Communications, and Sustainability (PACS) at Coca\-Cola leads the company’s efforts to engage with communities, media, and stakeholders while advancing our sustainability and social responsibility initiatives. We are committed to making a positive impact on society and the environment. **What will you do as a Coca\-Cola Ignite Intern?** * Gain hands\-on experience, develop critical skills, and handle complex projects with real business impact. * Get an opportunity accelerate your career growth within Coca\-Cola's global networked organization. * Network with leaders and colleagues, learning from their expertise through various engagement opportunities. * Be part of a vibrant global community of interns and early career professionals, sharing experiences and insights. * Contribute to meaningful initiatives promoting social impact and sustainability, making a positive impact in our communities **Key Responsibilities** As a PACS Intern, you will play an important role in supporting Coca\-Cola’s public relations, sustainability, and communication strategies. You will work on projects that promote environmental responsibility, foster community relations, and ensure that our brand’s message is communicated clearly and effectively. * Assist in developing and implementing public relations strategies that enhance Coca\-Cola’s reputation. * Support sustainability programs and corporate social responsibility (CSR) initiatives. * Monitor and track media coverage to assess public perception and reputation. * Help coordinate communication campaigns and community outreach programs. * Collaborate with internal teams to align sustainability efforts with company values. * Analyze stakeholder feedback and recommend improvements. **Requirements** * Educational background: An undergraduate student, pursuing a bachelor's degree, or master’s student, completing a master’s degree in communications, Public Affairs, Environmental Science, or related field. * Right to work: Legal authorisation to work in Ireland. No visa sponsorship is provided. * Commitment: Must be available to start October 1st, 2026, and commit to 36\.25 hours/week for a 12\-month internship. * Language: Fluency in English (with strong verbal and written communication skills) * Attributes: Enthusiasm towards Coca\-Cola, its brands and its values. Curious, agile, creative and passionate about learning and making a true impact in a high\-performance and diverse environment. * Relationship skills: Being inclusive, team\-oriented, and adept at building relationships. * Relevant Knowledge: Excellent writing and verbal communication skills. Passion for sustainability and community initiatives, with strong ethical standards. Strong understanding of sustainability practices and public relations. Proficiency in social media and content creation. title: Compliance Analyst
description: **Compliance Analyst** Our vision is to support the transition to a more sustainable energy system by helping to solve the commercial mechanics of a decarbonised market. We are an international, renewables\-focused energy trading and services business, established in 2011\. Since 2016, we have been part of a global corporate group with significant innovation and energy experience, allowing us to enhance and grow our client base and service offerings. We provide a comprehensive range of energy trading and market services designed to support the transition to a more sustainable energy system. By combining deep expertise in energy markets with cutting\-edge digital infrastructure, we help our clients maximise the value of their renewable assets, navigate market volatility, and contribute to system\-wide decarbonisation. We also operate a proprietary trading function, which is active in both spot and derivatives energy markets. At the core of our success is our people. Their commitment to our corporate values of excellence, curiosity, and humanity underpins every decision we make and helps drive positive outcomes for our clients. As we look to the future, our focus remains clear: to scale our impact, deepen our partnerships, and continue innovating for a carbon\-free system. The Position We are seeking a Compliance Analyst to join the compliance team. The successful candidate will report to the Compliance Manager. As a trading firm with a focus on energy commodities, the most relevant compliance areas are: * European financial regulations and directives such as EMIR 3\.0, MAR, and MiFID II. * Energy\-specific regulations such as REMIT. * Electricity and gas licences in Ireland, the U.K., and continental Europe. * Policies and procedures on conflicts of interest, data protection, competition, anti\-bribery, and whistleblowing. Compliance is a dynamic function which requires working with external and internal parties alike. Our diversified portfolio of activities, from client services to proprietary trading in financial markets, offers the successful candidate valuable experience and exciting career opportunities. The Role This is a varied and dynamic role, which will entail the following duties, including but not limited to: * Analysis of trade surveillance alerts, including monitoring and reviewing suspicious trading patterns. This will involve analysing large data sets and working closely with traders and analysts to understand trading strategies and determine whether they are compliant with regulations. * Regulatory reporting reconciliation, performing checks to ensure all regulatory reporting is completed accurately and on time. * Sanctions screening checks when onboarding new counterparties. * Ensuring regulatory filings are completed correctly and in a timely manner. * Maintaining the compliance issues register, investigating mitigation solutions, and assisting the compliance team in implementing those measures. * Responding to KYC or compliance requests from exchanges, brokers, counterparties, and other external stakeholders. * Supporting the team in delivering compliance training for staff. * Administering internal policies and procedures, keeping them up to date to reflect regulatory and organisational changes and informing relevant individuals of those changes. * Managing the communications surveillance system. The Successful Candidate Should Be Able To Demonstrate Required Qualifications * Minimum of 2 years’ experience in compliance, trade surveillance, risk, audit, middle office, back office, or regulatory advisory. * Honours degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting, or a similar subject. Skills \& Competencies * Practical, business\-enabling mindset with strong judgement and integrity. * Excellent analytical and research skills with great attention to detail. * Good knowledge of Microsoft 365, particularly Excel and PowerPoint. * Critical thinking and problem\-solving skills. * Flexibility and eagerness to learn new fields and develop new work practices. Desirable, but not essential * Professional Diploma in Compliance or willingness to take exams. * Knowledge of the electricity, gas, or wider energy commodity sectors. * Knowledge of derivatives markets, including futures and options, or financial regulations.

title: Customer Service/Sales Advisor
description: **1\. Purpose of the Role** The Customer Service \& Sales Advisor is the primary point of contact for all walk\-in customers, phone enquiries, and online orders at FixMyPC. This is a front\-of\-house role that combines customer service excellence with retail sales acumen, requiring someone who can confidently engage with customers, recommend appropriate tech solutions, manage repair bookings from intake to collection, and maintain the day\-to\-day operations of the shop floor. The ideal candidate is personable, tech\-savvy, commercially minded, and thrives in a fast\-paced environment. **2\. Key Responsibilities** **2\.1 Customer Service \& Front of House** * Greet every customer promptly and professionally; serve as the first point of contact for all in\-store visitors. * Listen to customer issues, diagnose problems at a high level, and advise on the best course of action (repair, replacement, accessory purchase, etc.). * Manage customer expectations regarding repair timelines, costs, and outcomes. * Handle customer complaints calmly and professionally, escalating to management where necessary. * Ensure every customer leaves with a positive impression of the FixMyPC brand. **2\.2 Repair Booking \& Follow\-Up** * Book in all repairs accurately, capturing device details, reported faults, customer contact information, and agreed pricing. * Assign repair tickets to technicians and track progress throughout the repair lifecycle. * Proactively follow up with customers via phone, text, or email to provide repair status updates. * Contact customers promptly when repairs are complete to arrange collection or delivery. * Chase outstanding collections and manage devices that have not been picked up within agreed timeframes. **2\.3 Sales \& Solution Selling** * Actively promote and upsell FixMyPC products and services including accessories, peripherals, refurbished devices, antivirus software, data backup solutions, and extended warranties. * Recommend appropriate technology solutions based on each customer’s needs and budget – not just what they ask for, but what will genuinely solve their problem. * Meet and exceed sales targets as set by management. * Stay current on product knowledge, new arrivals, and industry trends to confidently advise customers. * Process sales transactions accurately through the POS system. **2\.4 Phone \& Communication Management** * Answer all incoming phone calls promptly and professionally. * Handle phone\-based repair enquiries, provide quotes, and book in devices over the phone. * Return missed calls and respond to voicemails within the same business day. * Manage the shop’s email inbox, responding to customer queries in a timely manner. **2\.5 Online Orders \& Dispatch** * Process incoming online orders from the FixMyPC website and any third\-party marketplaces. * Pick, pack, and prepare orders for dispatch accurately and efficiently. * Arrange courier collections and print shipping labels. * Update order statuses and tracking information for customers. * Handle returns, refunds, and exchanges for online orders in line with company policy. **2\.6 Social Media \& Marketing Support** * Create and publish engaging posts on the FixMyPC Facebook page including promotions, tips, before/after repair showcases, and new stock arrivals. * Reshare relevant tech news, tips, and partner content to keep the page active and engaging. * Respond to Facebook messages and comments in a timely, professional manner. * Capture photos and short videos of repairs, products, and the shop for social media use. * Support any broader marketing campaigns or promotions as directed by management. **2\.7 Stock Management \& Shop Maintenance** * Conduct regular stock takes to maintain accurate inventory levels. * Receive, check, and shelve incoming deliveries. * Monitor stock levels and flag low\-stock or fast\-moving items to management for reordering. * Ensure product displays are well\-organised, clearly priced, and visually appealing. * Maintain the shop floor, counter area, and customer\-facing spaces to a high standard of cleanliness and tidiness at all times. * Carry out end\-of\-day closing procedures including cashing up, securing the premises, and general housekeeping. **3\. Person Specification** **3\.1 Essential Requirements** * Proven experience in a customer\-facing retail or sales role (minimum 1–2 years preferred). * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills – friendly, approachable, and articulate. * Strong sales ability with a natural instinct for solution selling and upselling. * Comfortable with technology: able to discuss laptops, PCs, phones, tablets, printers, and accessories with confidence. Not required to be a technician, but must understand the products well enough to advise customers. * Ability to think on your feet, work under pressure, and handle multiple tasks simultaneously in a busy shop environment. * Self\-motivated and capable of working independently with minimal supervision. * Strong attention to detail, especially when booking in repairs and processing orders. * Proficient with social media (Facebook in particular) – comfortable creating posts, taking photos, and engaging with an online audience. * Flexible to work weekends, bank holidays, and varied shift patterns as required by the business. * Full clean driving licence (preferred but not essential). **3\.2 Desirable Requirements** * Previous experience in a computer repair shop, electronics retail, or IT environment. * Familiarity with POS systems and CRM/repair tracking software. * Experience with e\-commerce order fulfilment and dispatch. * Knowledge of basic troubleshooting (e.g., identifying whether a device needs a screen replacement, battery swap, or software reset). * Experience managing or contributing to a business social media page. * Knowledge of data protection and GDPR as it relates to handling customer devices and personal data. **4\. Key Competencies** **4\.1 Customer Focus** Consistently puts the customer first. Builds rapport quickly, listens actively, and ensures every interaction leaves the customer feeling valued and well\-served. **4\.2 Commercial Awareness** Understands the importance of driving revenue. Identifies sales opportunities naturally during customer interactions and contributes to meeting business targets. **4\.3 Adaptability \& Resilience** Comfortable in a dynamic environment where priorities shift throughout the day. Handles pressure, multitasks effectively, and maintains composure during busy periods or difficult customer interactions. **4\.4 Communication** Communicates clearly and confidently in person, on the phone, and in writing (including social media). Keeps customers informed proactively and manages expectations with honesty and professionalism. **4\.5 Initiative \& Ownership** Doesn’t wait to be told – sees what needs to be done and does it. Takes ownership of the shop floor and customer experience as if it were their own business. **4\.6 Teamwork** Works collaboratively with technicians and management. Shares information, supports colleagues during busy periods, and contributes to a positive working atmosphere. **5\. Working Conditions** * Based on\-site at FixMyPC, Unit 2, Church Street, Kilcock, Co. Kildare. * Standard retail hours with flexibility required for weekend and bank holiday cover. * Standing for extended periods on the shop floor. * Occasional lifting of stock and equipment (e.g., PCs, monitors, printers). **6\. How to Apply** Interested candidates should submit a CV and brief cover letter to: **Email:** info@fixmypc.ie **Subject Line:** Application – Customer Service \& Sales Advisor *FixMyPC is an equal opportunities employer.* Job Types: Full\-time, Part\-time, Permanent, Fixed term, Specified\-purpose, Internship, Graduate Contract length: 12 months Pay: €27,000\.00\-€50,835\.35 per year Expected hours: 20\.0 – 38\.0 per week Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee discount * Flexitime * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Customer Success Agent
description: Lanas is igniting healthcare innovation. Our technology is used by millions of clinical users in over twenty countries. From core clinical workflows to AI\-driven innovation, our products help reduce admin, improve access, drive efficiencies, and support safer, more connected care. **Position:** ------------- We have an exciting opportunity for a Customer Success Agent to join our busy Customer Service Team, supporting our RxWeb product. Further, you can also expect:** * Company matched pension scheme. * 22 days annual leave, increasing yearly up to 26 days. * Enhanced maternity/paternity benefits. * Flexible working/work from home opportunities. * 2 paid CSR Volunteering/Charity Days per year. * 1 Wellbeing Day per year. * Full access to our Employee Assistance Programme. * Regular wellbeing supports and webinars. * Annual leave purchase scheme. * Working abroad opportunities – 20 working days per year. ***Lanas is an equal opportunities employer.***

title: Data Analyst
description: **About The Role** We are seeking a highly analytical and detail\-oriented Data Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will be responsible for transforming complex data into understandable and conclusive insights. You will play a key role in training different business areas on the effective use of our Business Intelligence (BI) tools and advising them on how to maximize the value derived from our data. If you have a passion for data storytelling, strong communication skills, and enjoy empowering others with data, we encourage you to apply. **Responsibilities** * Collect, clean, analyze, and interpret large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights. * Create clear, concise, and impactful visualizations, reports, and dashboards that effectively communicate findings to various stakeholders. * Develop and deliver training sessions to educate different business units on the functionality and best practices of BI tools. * Provide expert advice and guidance to the business area on how to leverage data to answer key business questions and drive informed decision\-making. * Collaborate with data engineers and other teams to ensure data accuracy, accessibility, and consistency. * Identify opportunities for process improvements and data optimization. * Stay up\-to\-date with industry best practices and emerging trends in data analysis, BI tools, and data visualization. **Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, Business Analytics, or a Business related discipline. * Proven experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar analytical role. * Strong proficiency in SQL. * Extensive experience with Business Intelligence (BI) tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Looker, Qlik Sense). * Excellent data visualization skills and the ability to present complex information clearly and concisely. * Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and critical thinking abilities. * Exceptional communication and presentation skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non\-technical audiences. * Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast\-paced environment. * Detail\-oriented with a strong commitment to data accuracy and integrity. During a trip to Thailand, Pedro and Lidia, the founders of Holafly, experienced the frustration of not having an internet connection. Inspired by their own experiences, they came up with a simple but brilliant idea: why not allow travellers to buy data plans for any destination from their home country? Our passion for providing easy and convenient connectivity to travellers around the world drives everything we do. We love our work and we do it with pride. Stay connected wherever you go.

title: EMEA Employee Relations Coordinator (Entry-Level)
description: **Overview** Fisher Investments Ireland is searching for an EMEA Employee Relations Coordinator who is looking to get their “foot\-in\-the\-door” and learn more about the Employee Relations function. The Employee Relations function is focused on providing support and advice to employees and managers on a range of people issues, including but not limited to absence, wellbeing, disciplinaries and performance. **The Opportunity:** The Coordinator works within the EMEA Employee Relations and Onboarding team to assist the firm with various tasks, ensures a timely response to internal and external questions, and provides feedback to the department regarding process and service improvements. You will be working side\-by\-side with all members of the team and departments, assisting with various onboarding, administrative, and select employee related matters. The Associate will be responsible for assisting with various items tied to the employment lifecycle and all groups/mangers across the organisation. As a member of the Employee Relations and Onboarding team, you will have a real impact on the success of supporting our internal clients in the office and our expansion efforts. You will report directly to the EMEA Employee Relations Manager. **The Day\-to\-Day:** * First point of contact for all employees/managers to answer questions on Company policies and procedures * Be responsible for the administration of the ER and Onboarding functions * Maintain data and overall upkeep of the group’s records * Provide ad\-hoc support to team members on HR projects / initiatives * Identify elevations and route to or work directly with the appropriate team member or group * Support the EMEA ER team with note taking in select meetings * Assist with employee onboarding, reviewing applications, conducting background checks, drafting contract of employment and contractor agreements * Serve as a point of contact for various onboarding questions and right to work checks * Identify potential process improvements and recommends solutions * Assist the group by drafting Company policies and procedures * Complete other administrative duties as necessary **Your Qualifications:** * University degree or equivalent combination of education and experience required * High level of attention to detail * Ability to adapt to changes in a fast paced, team environment * Excellent communication skills * Ability to prioritize workload and manage time across various tasks * Proficient in Microsoft Office software * Desire and ability to function in a team format * Able to display ownership and self\-motivation * Exceptional organisation, prioritisation and time management skills * Highly reliable and accurate **Why Fisher Investments Ireland:** The global Fisher organisation distinguishes itself by putting clients first, providing unmatched service, and taking a personalised approach to investing. You can feel confident knowing that Fisher Investments and Fisher Investments Ireland align with our clients’ priorities by using a simple and transparent fee structure and recognised European custodians. It’s the people that make the Fisher purpose possible, and to help our employees meet their long\-term goals, we offer an array of benefits, including: * 100% paid premiums for our top\-tier supplemental healthcare and dental plans for employees and their qualified dependents * 28 days annual leave, with the ability to purchase up to 3 additional days per year, plus up to 11 paid holidays * Enhanced maternity pay package with 26 weeks’ top up to full base pay for eligible employees * $10,000\* fertility, hormonal health and family\-forming benefit * A retirement pension plan, featuring a 9% company pension contribution of your base pay and an additional company match of up to 5% of base pay * Monthly gym subsidy of up to €50 per month * Employee Assistance Program and other emotional wellbeing services * A collaborative working environment that practices ongoing training, educational support and employee appreciation events * This is an in\-office role. Based on your role, tenure, and performance eligibility you may have the opportunity to participate in our hybrid work from home program. This program is subject to change. * Employees residing outside of the US will be eligible for the $10,000 equivalent in their local currency. FISHER INVESTMENTS IRELAND IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

title: Economist
description: 8 Apr 2026 Dublin, IE, IE **Economist – Investment Banking** **About Goodbody** Goodbody is a leading financial services firm, committed to serving and growing client relationships for 150 years. We are a full service, investment\-led business offering wealth management, asset management, investment banking and private equity services. Our staff of over 400 work in offices across Dublin, London, Cork and Galway. We’re proud to offer prospective employees a professional, collegial work environment that encourages diversity of thought and background. Our reputation in the Irish market for putting customers first is as consistent as the company’s long\-standing commitment to giving back to the communities that we work in. **About the Economist Role:** The role will report directly to the Chief Economist within the Research team of the Investment Banking department. The Economics function has responsibilities with a number of areas within Goodbody, including Equity Capital Markets, Debt Capital Markets, Corporate Advisory and Wealth Management. It is anticipated that the successful candidate will have a key involvement in all of these areas. The work includes analysis, forecasting, commentary, client communications and presenting. The role is challenging and offers significant scope for career development. The role will involve travel over time **Economist Accountabilities include:** * Analysis and support of the Irish \& UK economies, including taking a lead role over time in agreed sectors/areas * Data analysis in conjunction with the Data Analytics team * Timely delivery of economic publications and presentations * Communication, written and verbal, of economic material to clients and the media * Managing relationships of key stakeholders, internally and externally **Qualifications and Experience** * Proficient in the use of Microsoft Office programmes * Data coding languages (Python, R) would be advantageous * Financial Services experience would be beneficial but not crucial. * Experience in an economics role would be beneficial * It is anticipated that the successful individual will have a strong academic record, preferably with a post graduate degree in Economics * No specific industry qualifications are required, but an interest in and knowledge of financial markets is an advantage **Ideal Characteristics** * A keen interest in economics \& markets * Outstanding presentation and communication skills * The ability to write and produce reports to an excellent standard * Excellent analytical skills with the ability to work to tight deadlines * A record of achievement in their career to date including relevant work experience in this field * Drive and initiative **Employee Value Proposition** Goodbody is focused on helping our employees thrive, and we do this by bringing to life our Employee Value Proposition (EVP) ‘Together we prosper’. Our firm’s purpose is to create long\-term prosperity, and we cannot execute on our purpose without the right talent at Goodbody. To make sure we attract and retain industry\-leading, passionate and inspiring talent, we’ve built a vibrant culture based on inclusion, opportunities to grow and a commitment to ‘do the right thing’, one of our core values. We live this by offering: * Regular employee events through our universal inclusion programme fostering a sense of belonging and celebrating differences. And an ‘always on’ approach to social, financial, mental and physical wellbeing. * A benefits package that includes a generous pension scheme, educational sponsorship and health insurance, along with a hybrid working model to promote better work/life balance * Opportunities to give back through sustainability initiatives, volunteering and on\-site and online meetings with our charity partners Goodbody is an equal opportunities employer and is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace which values and benefits from the diversity of our workforce. Reasonable accommodations are offered at every stage of our recruitment process. Should you have a reasonable accommodation request please include a note in your application or email us directly at HR@Goodbody.ie Please read the Goodbody Recruitment Privacy Notice. By submitting your personal data to us, you acknowledge that you have read and understood this Privacy Notice and agree to the use of your personal data in line with this. We will only use the information that we collect about you lawfully in accordance with the Data Protection Acts, 1988 and 2003 and the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. **Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to Goodbody by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role.** Goodbody operates a direct sourcing model and where agency assistance is required, the HR team will engage directly with our recruitment partners. Goodbody Stockbrokers UC, trading as Goodbody, is regulated by the Central Bank of Ireland. In the UK, Goodbody is also subject to regulation by the Financial Conduct Authority. Goodbody is a member of Euronext Dublin and the London Stock Exchange. Goodbody is a member of the group of companies headed by AIB Group plc. For AIB Employees: Please note there are specific controls in place for transfers between AIB and Goodbody. Goodbody is part of AIB Group and you must inform your AIB line manager and HR BP in advance of making an application to Goodbody. **Job Segment:** Investment Banking, Private Equity, Data Analyst, Equity, Banking, Finance, Data

title: Energy Analyst
description: **Wind Analysis Specialist (Renewables)** Location: **Newbridge, Co. Kildare, Ireland** (Hybrid working model with blended **working from home for three days** and **two days in the office** ) Duration: **Permanent** Reporting to: **Engineering Manager** Our client is an Irish Semi‑State Organisation, headquartered in Newbridge, Co. Kildare, has evolved into a modern **Climate‑Action Enterprise** . It now focuses on **Renewable Energy Development** , rehabilitation, waste and resource recovery, and **Large‑Scale Environmental Restoration projects.** Its mission centres on supporting Ireland’s transition to a Climate‑Neutral Economy by 2050 through **Clean‑Energy Solutions** , Biodiversity Regeneration, and Sustainable Land Management. They are hiring a **Wind Analysis Specialist** . The purpose of this role is to support the **Engineering team** in an ongoingpipeline of **RenewableProjects** in termsof **Wind Analysis** and **Resource Assessment.** This role will involve working with the engineering **Team Designing Windfarms,** assessing **Site Constraints, WindProfiling, Turbine Site Optimisation** and engaging with internal/external stakeholders as required at a project level. The role will also involve completing **Wind Farm Layout Designs** , **GIS Mapping, Energy Yield Assessments,** and **Project Optimisation** to overcome constraints identified for projects in the planning phase of a development. It will ensure the successful delivery of key projects in the **Renewable** space. Success in this role will be measured by quality **Wind Layout Designs,** **Quality Resource Assessments** , timely delivery of projects, cost savings, improved team performance and contribution to the overall success of the department and organisation. The main duties and responsibilities of the role will include the following: * **Windfarm Analysis:** * Produce **Windfarm Layouts** using **WindPro** or similar software like **Windfarmer** or **Openwind** * Working with the engineering team colleagues to optimise solutions to **Infrastructure Design.** * Assessing **GIS mapping** and constraints for the **Wind Farm, Solar Farm** , and other **Renewable Energy Projects.** * Contribute to various **Wind Farm Studies** including **Telecommunications,** **Noise, Shadow Flicker** and **Visual ImpactAssessments** to aid the development and operation of projects. * Produce **Solar Layouts** using **PVsyst** or similar software. * **Wind Appraisal Resource Assessment:** * Assist with the **Field Deployment** and **Monitoring** of remote sensing installations for resource assessment measurements for the project pipeline, including **Wind** ( **mast and LiDAR)** and **Solar Measurement.** * Checking, management and **Analysis** of recorded data as required across the **Project Portfolio** including initial **Project Energy Yield Estimates.** * Complete **Site Suitability Studies** to characterise sites and develop project layout options, including working with **external Consultants** to validate and modify as necessary. * **Analyse** and **Optimise Wind Resource** and **Energy Yield Predictions** for new, developing, and **Operational Wind Farms.** * **Site Condition Assessments** for **Pre\-Tender Information** and input for **Turbine Selection.** * Contribute to the procurement of external services related to **Turbine Supply Agreements, Wind Data Management, Resource Assessment, Site Suitability Assessments** and curtailment analysis(noise, shadow flicker)associated with company related projects. * Provide technical support for the due diligence of their investments in **Renewable Energy Projects** , including review of **Consultant Reports** and specification of third party analysis. * Prepare and/or review **Scope of Work** for third party services for project resource assessment and fully participate in the various stages of the associated procurement processes. Support the monitoring and evaluation of the performance of principle consultants with respect to their use on future projects. * Ensure that all works carried out under your remit comply with their Health, Safety and Environmental policies and all relevant EU and National policies and statutory requirements. * All employees are expected to co\-operate fully with all provisions taken by the company for ensuring Health and Safety, and Welfare of co\-employees and members of the public using the premises. * Proactively participate in the **Performance Management** process to ensure delivery of own, and the wider team's objectives. **The Person:** The ideal candidate will possess the following qualification, skills, knowledge, and attributes: * Level 8 qualification required in engineering, science or relevant discipline for **Wind Analysis.** * Minimum four years relevant post\-graduate experience. * Experience using specialist software for **Wind Layout Design** such as **WindPro** is essential. * Experience conducting **Geospatial Analysis** and geoprocessing tasks using **Arcgis Pro,ǪGIS, Mapinfo** or similar. * Knowledge of **Data Analysis** using advanced **MS Excel, Matlab, Python, R** or similar. * Knowledge of using specialist software for **Solar Layout Design** such as **PVsyst** or similar desirable but not essential. * Strong analytical and IT skills. * Ability to communicate clearly with other specialists, professionals and management not from a technology background. * Good interpersonal and teamwork skills with capability to work collaboratively in a multidisciplinary team. * Ability to balance attention to detail with engineering judgment. * Positive attitude, flexibility and willingness to tackle new areas of work beyond core skill. **Other Benefits Include:** Employer matched pension contributions They invest in their people and have a strong commitment to your development. They provide free, confidential counselling \& wellbeing support service that provides in\-the\-moment support to you. It also provides access to self\-guided meditation, Fitness \& Nutrition Pathways, Digital Gym \& on demand webinars. They fund subscriptions for work\-related professional bodies. Interested? Please send a copy of your CV to **hello@enexa\-partners.com**

title: Engineering & Science Intern – 1 year programme,
description: Do you want to join us in helping to fight the world's most threatening diseases and enabling access to care for more people around the world? At Siemens Healthineers, we pioneer breakthroughs in healthcare. For everyone. Everywhere. Sustainably. We offer you a flexible and dynamic environment with opportunities to go beyond your comfort zone in order to grow personally and professionally. Sound interesting? Then come and join our global team as **Engineering \& Science Intern – 1 year programme** , at our facility in Swords, Dublin. This is a role well suited to an ambitious student pursuing an engineering or science degree, working under the guidance of senior engineers to support various projects and learn practical engineering principles **Your tasks and responsibilities:** Gain practical experience in engineering tasks and project workflows Support design verification and validation activities, including test setup and data collection Exposure to design, development and testing of medical devices Collaborate with cross\-functional teams (Quality, Regulatory, Manufacturing) to ensure compliance Your qualifications and experience: Currently enrolled in an engineering/science discipline (e.g., Mechanical, Electrical, Industrial, Manufacturing, Material Science, Chemistry etc ) **Your attributes and skills:** Strong academic performance with solid understanding of science and engineering principles Adaptability – Ability to learn quickly in a regulated environment. Excellent problem\-solving and analytical skills **Our global team:** We are a team of more than 76,000 highly dedicated employees across more than 70 countries passionately pushing the boundaries of what’s possible in healthcare to help improve people’s lives around the world. As a leader in the industry, we aspire to create better outcomes and experiences for patients no matter where they live or what health issues they are facing. Our portfolio, spanning from in\-vitro and in\-vivo diagnostics to image\-guided therapy and innovative cancer care, is crucial for clinical decision\-making and treatment pathways. **Our culture:** We are part of an incredible community of scientists, clinicians, developers, researchers, professionals, and skilled specialists pushing the boundaries of what’s possible, to improve people’s lives around the world. We embrace a culture of inclusivity in which the power and potential of every individual can be unleashed. We spark ideas that lead to positive impact and continued success. As an equal opportunity employer, we welcome applications from individuals with disabilities.

title: Executive Associate (Strategy, Data & Delivery)
description: The Executive Associate, DCEO Office (Operations \& Insights) is a high‑impact, early‑career role that blends executive support, data analysis, project delivery, and board‑level storytelling. Partnering directly with the DCEO, you will orchestrate the rhythm of the business, secure timely, high‑quality inputs from functions and service providers, and transform complex data into crisp narratives and decision materials. The role flexes with the CEO’s priorities week‑to‑week, ensuring momentum, clarity, and results. **Key Responsibilities** * Run the DCEO Rhythm of the Business: own the annual, quarterly, monthly, and weekly cadence, including inputs, deadlines, and deliverables. * Board \& ExComm reporting: shape storylines, synthesise metrics, and produce clear, visually compelling packs that “bring the requisite audience on a journey” without overloading figures. * Insight generation: translate operational and financial data into trends, risks, opportunities, and recommended actions. * Data pipeline coordination: secure timely, complete, and high‑quality data from functions and external service providers; resolve gaps and inconsistencies fast. * Presentation excellence: build executive‑level slides and visuals in PowerPoint, uphold a consistent design system and narrative structure. * Project management: plan, track, and deliver DCEO‑sponsored initiatives, manage scope and comms. * Stakeholder orchestration: align C\-Suite colleagues, Directors, Finance, HR, Medical, Clinical/Ops, IT, and external partners around DCEO priorities. * Decision support: prepare succinct decision memos (“one‑pagers”), options, trade‑offs, and clear recommendations. * Executive briefings: package pre‑reads and talking points for internal and external meetings; ensure pre‑wire with key stakeholders. * Follow‑through: capture meeting actions, their owners, and due dates, then drive to closure and surface slippage early. * Issue escalation: spot signals in sow downs or failures, escalate the right issues to the DCEO with context and proposed fixes. * Confidentiality \& governance: handle sensitive information with discretion, follow information‑security and approvals protocols. * Calendar and priority triage: protect DCEO focus time, sequence work to match shifting priorities. * Vendor/service‑provider management: set expectations, SLAs, and delivery quality for data and content inputs. * KPI / dashboard upkeep: maintain a concise “single source of truth” for top metrics and update cadence. * Change agility: rapidly re‑plan when priorities move, communicate impacts and next steps. * Process improvement: simplify reporting cycles and automate where practical (templates, checklists, light scripts). * Internal communications: draft professional crisp updates from DCEO office as required. * Knowledge management: maintain a searchable, version‑controlled repository of past packs, decisions, and templates. * Culture \& standards: model pace, professionalism, and high bar for quality across all deliverables. **Person Requirements** * Early‑career track record (typically 2–4 years) in one or more of: analytics, strategy/ops, PMO, management consulting, Human Resources or high‑performing EA/Chief‑of‑Staff‑associate roles. * Outstanding PowerPoint: visual storytelling, executive‑level slide craft, and narrative design. * Strong quantitative literacy: Excel/Sheets for analysis; comfort with BI outputs (Power BI/Tableau) and manipulating datasets. * Project delivery: plans, stakeholder management, and a reputation for closing actions. * Executive presence \& writing: concise briefs, decision memos, and clear meeting pre‑reads. * Stakeholder savvy: confident with senior leaders, can secure inputs without formal authority. * Bias for action: self‑starter, organised, and calm under shifting priorities and short notice. * High integrity \& discretion handling confidential information. **Desirable Requirements** * Exposure to board reporting or investor‑style narratives. * Power BI/Tableau basics; familiarity with SharePoint/OneDrive for structured content management. * PMP/PRINCE2, Agile foundations, or analytics/data‑viz coursework/certifications. * Experience working with external vendors/service providers and SLAs. **Qualifications** * Bachelor’s degree (business, economics, analytics, engineering, or related) or equivalent practical experience. THE MATER PRIVATE IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES EMPLOYER

title: FP&A Associate | Aviation Finance | Dublin
description: **FP\&A Associate \| Aviation Finance \| Dublin** A leading aviation platform is seeking to appoint an **FP\&A Associate** to join its finance team in Dublin. The position offers a competitive salary and benefits package, along with strong career development opportunities. **Application Process** If you are interested in discussing this opportunity further, please apply online or contact Karinda at GKR Search \& Selection for a confidential conversation. **\#AircraftLeasing \#AviationFinance \#FPandA \#FinancialModelling \#AviationJobs \#FinanceJobs \#DublinJobs \#IrelandJobs \#GKRSearchAndSelection**

title: Financial Analyst | Aircraft Leasing | Dublin
description: **Financial Analyst \| Aircraft Leasing \| Dublin** A leading global aviation finance platform is seeking to appoint a Financial Analyst to join its team in Dublin. This role offers the opportunity to work closely with complex financing structures supporting a large international portfolio of commercial aircraft leased to airlines worldwide. The successful candidate will play an important role in analysing financing arrangements, preparing lender and investor reporting, and supporting the ongoing management of debt facilities linked to aircraft assets. The position offers excellent exposure to structured finance in a fast\-paced, highly analytical environment. **Key Responsibilities** * Perform financial analysis across a range of structured and corporate financing arrangements * Prepare monthly and quarterly reporting packages for lenders and investment partners * Analyse financing structures, including debt balances, fees, and distribution calculations * Support activities related to borrowing requests, facility releases, and loan settlements * Maintain and update financial models and large datasets used in reporting and analysis * Assist with cash reconciliations and portfolio data analysis * Review and interpret financing documentation and related contractual terms * Support ad\-hoc analytical projects and reporting requests **Candidate Profile** * Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or a related analytical discipline * 2\+ years’ experience in FP\&A, accounting, investment banking, or a similar analytical finance role * Strong Excel and financial modelling skills * Excellent analytical and problem\-solving capability with strong attention to detail * Ability to manage multiple deadlines and prioritise work effectively * Experience working with financial datasets and reporting tools * Exposure to aviation finance or asset\-backed financing structures is advantageous **The Opportunity** This is a hybrid, full\-time permanent role offering strong exposure to complex financing structures within the aviation sector. The position provides an excellent opportunity to develop financial modelling and analytical expertise within a global and collaborative team environment. **Application Process** If you are interested in discussing this opportunity further, please apply online or contact Karinda Tolland at GKR Search \& Selection for a confidential conversation. Following your application for this role, GKR may contact you about other positions we believe may be suitable for you. If you do not wish to be contacted about opportunities, please let us know. For more information, please refer to our privacy statement available on our website. **\#Finance \#AircraftLeasing \#AviationFinance \#FinancialAnalyst \#StructuredFinance \#AssetFinance \#AviationJobs \#DublinJobs \#LeasingCareers**

title: Fire Sprinkler Project Engineer
